---
title: "glm_script"
output: html_document
date: "2025-11-26"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyverse)
library(tidymodels)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)
library(Matrix)
library(pbkrtest)
library(broom.mixed)
emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 10000)
emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 10000)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
select_best_lmer <- function(data, 
                             fixed_effects,
                             conserved_random_effects = NULL,
                             test_random_effects = NULL,   # ← default NULL
                             criterion = "AIC",
                             REML = FALSE,
                             verbose = TRUE) {

  # Build conserved random effects string
  if (!is.null(conserved_random_effects) && length(conserved_random_effects) > 0) {
    conserved_re_str <- paste0("(1|", conserved_random_effects, ")", collapse = " + ")
    base_formula <- paste(fixed_effects, "+", conserved_re_str)
  } else {
    base_formula <- fixed_effects
    conserved_re_str <- ""
  }

  ctrl <- lmerControl(autoscale = TRUE)

  # ── KEY CHANGE: handle empty/NULL test_random_effects ──────────────────
  # If nothing to test, just fit the base formula once
  if (is.null(test_random_effects) || length(test_random_effects) == 0) {
    
    if (verbose) {
      cat("No test random effects provided — fitting base formula only\n")
      cat("Base formula:", base_formula, "\n\n")
    }
    
    model_formula <- as.formula(base_formula)
    
    model_fit <- tryCatch({
      lmer(model_formula, data = data, REML = FALSE, control = ctrl)
    }, error = function(e) {
      if (verbose) cat("Model failed:", e$message, "\n")
      return(NULL)
    }, warning = function(w) {
      if (verbose) cat("Model warning:", w$message, "\n")
      lmer(model_formula, data = data, REML = FALSE, control = ctrl)
    })
    
    if (is.null(model_fit)) stop("Base model failed to converge!")
    
    conv_check <- if (is.null(model_fit@optinfo$conv$lme4$messages)) {
      TRUE
    } else {
      !any(grepl("failed to converge", model_fit@optinfo$conv$lme4$messages))
    }
    
    best_model_info <- tibble(
      model_num      = 1L,
      random_effects = "",
      n_random       = 0L,
      AIC            = AIC(model_fit),
      BIC            = BIC(model_fit),
      logLik         = as.numeric(logLik(model_fit)),
      converged      = conv_check,
      formula        = base_formula
    )
    
    if (verbose) {
      cat("Model fitting complete!\n\nBest model by", criterion, ":\n")
      print(best_model_info)
      cat("\n")
    }
    
    best_model_ml <- lmer(model_formula, data = data, REML = FALSE, control = ctrl)
    best_model    <- lmer(model_formula, data = data, REML = TRUE, control = ctrl)
    
    if (verbose) {
      cat("Best model formula:\n")
      print(formula(best_model))
      cat("\nComputing KR F-tests...\n")
    }
    
    interaction_test <- tryCatch({
      anova(best_model, ddf = "Kenward-Roger")
    }, error = function(e) {
      if (verbose) cat("KR failed, falling back to Satterthwaite:", e$message, "\n")
      anova(best_model, ddf = "Satterthwaite")
    })
    
    if (verbose) {
      cat("Omnibus F-tests:\n")
      print(interaction_test)
      cat("\n")
    }
    
    return(list(
      best_model       = best_model,
      best_model_ml    = best_model_ml,
      interaction_test = interaction_test,
      model_results    = best_model_info,
      best_model_info  = best_model_info,
      all_results      = best_model_info
    ))
  }
  # ── END KEY CHANGE ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  # Generate all possible combinations including "none"
  all_combinations <- lapply(0:length(test_random_effects), function(n) {
    if (n == 0) list(character(0))
    else combn(test_random_effects, n, simplify = FALSE)
  }) %>% unlist(recursive = FALSE)

  if (verbose) {
    cat("Testing", length(all_combinations), "random effects combinations\n")
    cat("Base formula:", base_formula, "\n\n")
  }

  # Initialize results storage
  model_results <- tibble(
    model_num      = integer(),
    random_effects = character(),
    n_random       = integer(),
    AIC            = numeric(),
    BIC            = numeric(),
    logLik         = numeric(),
    converged      = logical(),
    formula        = character()
  )

  # Fit all models
  for (i in seq_along(all_combinations)) {

    test_re_terms <- if (length(all_combinations[[i]]) == 0) {
      ""
    } else {
      paste0("(1|", all_combinations[[i]], ")", collapse = " + ")
    }

    formula_str <- if (test_re_terms == "") {
      base_formula
    } else {
      paste(base_formula, "+", test_re_terms)
    }

    model_formula <- as.formula(formula_str)
    
    model_fit <- tryCatch({
      lmer(model_formula, data = data, REML = REML, control = ctrl)
    }, error = function(e) {
      if (verbose) cat("Model", i, "failed:", e$message, "\n")
      return(NULL)
    }, warning = function(w) {
      if (verbose) cat("Model", i, "warning:", w$message, "\n")
      lmer(model_formula, data = data, REML = REML, control = ctrl)
    })
    
    if (!is.null(model_fit)) {
      conv_check <- if (is.null(model_fit@optinfo$conv$lme4$messages)) {
        TRUE
      } else {
        !any(grepl("failed to converge", model_fit@optinfo$conv$lme4$messages))
      }
      
      model_results <- model_results %>%
        add_row(
          model_num      = i,
          random_effects = paste(all_combinations[[i]], collapse = " + "),
          n_random       = length(all_combinations[[i]]),
          AIC            = AIC(model_fit),
          BIC            = BIC(model_fit),
          logLik         = as.numeric(logLik(model_fit)),
          converged      = conv_check,
          formula        = formula_str
        )
    }
    
    if (verbose && i %% 50 == 0) {
      cat("Fitted", i, "of", length(all_combinations), "models\n")
    }
  }
  
  if (verbose) cat("\nModel fitting complete!\n\n")
  
  model_results_filtered <- model_results %>%
    filter(converged) %>%
    arrange(!!sym(criterion))
  
  best_model_info <- model_results_filtered %>% slice(1)
  
  if (nrow(best_model_info) == 0) stop("No models converged successfully!")
  
  if (verbose) {
    cat("Best model by", criterion, ":\n")
    print(best_model_info)
    cat("\n")
  }
  
  best_formula   <- as.formula(best_model_info$formula)
  best_model_ml  <- lmer(best_formula, data = data, REML = FALSE, control = ctrl)
  best_model     <- lmer(best_formula, data = data, REML = TRUE, control = ctrl)

  if (verbose) {
    cat("Best model formula:\n")
    print(formula(best_model))
    cat("\nComputing KR F-tests...\n")
  }

  interaction_test <- tryCatch({
    anova(best_model, ddf = "Kenward-Roger")
  }, error = function(e) {
    if (verbose) cat("KR failed, falling back to Satterthwaite:", e$message, "\n")
    anova(best_model, ddf = "Satterthwaite")
  })

  if (verbose) {
    cat("Omnibus F-tests:\n")
    print(interaction_test)
    cat("\n")
  }

  return(list(
    best_model       = best_model,
    best_model_ml    = best_model_ml,
    interaction_test = interaction_test,
    model_results    = model_results_filtered,
    best_model_info  = best_model_info,
    all_results      = model_results
  ))
}

analyze_and_plot_contrasts <- function(model,
                                       interaction_test,
                                       plot_title,
                                       event_colors,
                                       y_label = "Fitted Values",
                                       x_var = "event",
                                       x_var2 = NULL,
                                       band_var = "band",
                                       alpha = 0.05) {
  #' Analyze contrasts and create plots based on interaction significance
  #'
  #' @param model lmer model object (REML=TRUE)
  #' @param interaction_test anova table from anova(model, ddf="Kenward-Roger")
  #' @param plot_title Character string for main plot title
  #' @param event_colors Named vector mapping event levels to colors
  #' @param y_label Character string for y-axis label
  #' @param x_var Character string for main predictor variable name (default "event")
  #' @param x_var2 Optional second focal predictor (e.g. "condition"); enables two/three-way screening
  #' @param band_var Character string for band variable name (default "band")
  #' @param alpha Significance level for omnibus F-test gate (default 0.05)
  #'
  #' @return List with: plot (ggplot object), contrasts (tibble), plot_type (character)

  # Create formula string for subtitle
  model_formula_str <- paste(deparse(formula(model)), collapse = " ")
  model_formula_str <- gsub("\\s+", " ", model_formula_str)

  # Helper: find interaction term in anova table regardless of variable order
  find_interaction_term <- function(vars, anova_rownames) {
    candidates <- anova_rownames
    for (v in vars) {
      candidates <- candidates[grepl(paste0("(^|:)", v, "($|:)"), candidates)]
    }
    candidates <- candidates[lengths(strsplit(candidates, ":")) == length(vars)]
    if (length(candidates) == 1) candidates else NA_character_
  }

  # Helper: add sig stars after BH adjustment
  add_sig_stars <- function(df) {
    df$p.value.adjusted <- p.adjust(df$p.value, method = "BH")
    df %>% mutate(sig = case_when(
      p.value.adjusted < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value.adjusted < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.value.adjusted < 0.05  ~ "*",
      p.value.adjusted < 0.1   ~ ".",
      TRUE                     ~ ""
    ))
  }

  # Get augmented data
  model_data <- augment(model)

  # If x_var is numeric, coerce to factor for plotting and record levels for emmeans `at`
  x_var_at <- NULL
  if (is.numeric(model_data[[x_var]])) {
    x_var_at  <- list(sort(unique(model_data[[x_var]])))
    names(x_var_at) <- x_var
    model_data <- model_data %>% mutate(!!sym(x_var) := factor(!!sym(x_var)))
  }

  # ---- CASE: x_var2 provided — screen for two-way and three-way interactions ----
  if (!is.null(x_var2)) {

    three_way_term <- find_interaction_term(c(band_var, x_var, x_var2), rownames(interaction_test))
    two_way_term   <- find_interaction_term(c(x_var, x_var2),           rownames(interaction_test))

    three_way_sig <- !is.na(three_way_term) && interaction_test[three_way_term, "Pr(>F)"] < alpha
    two_way_sig   <- !is.na(two_way_term)   && interaction_test[two_way_term,   "Pr(>F)"] < alpha

    if (three_way_sig) {

      cat("Three-way", paste0(band_var, ":", x_var, ":", x_var2), "interaction is significant\n")

      emm_formula   <- as.formula(paste0("~ ", x_var, " * ", x_var2, " * ", band_var))
      contrasts_emm <- emmeans(model, emm_formula, at = x_var_at)
      raw_contrasts <- contrast(contrasts_emm,
                                simple = list(x_var, x_var2),
                                method = "pairwise",
                                adjust = "none")
      contrast_tests <- do.call(rbind, summary(raw_contrasts)) %>%
        as_tibble() %>%
        add_sig_stars()

      print(contrast_tests)

      contrasts_sig <- contrast_tests %>% filter(sig != "")

      if (nrow(contrasts_sig) > 0) {
        sig_bands <- unique(contrasts_sig[[band_var]])
        plot_data <- model_data %>% filter(!!sym(band_var) %in% sig_bands)

        p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = !!sym(band_var), y = .fitted, fill = !!sym(x_var))) +
          geom_boxplot(position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), alpha = 0.7) +
          stat_summary(fun = median, geom = "point", size = 3,
                       position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
                       shape = 95, show.legend = FALSE) +
          stat_summary(fun.data = function(x) {
            data.frame(ymin = quantile(x, 0.25), ymax = quantile(x, 0.75))
          }, geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2,
          position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), show.legend = FALSE) +
          geom_point(position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.2, dodge.width = 0.9),
                     alpha = 0.5, size = 1.5) +
          facet_wrap(as.formula(paste0("~ ", x_var2))) +
          scale_fill_manual(values = event_colors) +
          theme_minimal() +
          labs(x = "Frequency Band", y = y_label,
               fill = stringr::str_to_title(x_var),
               title = paste0(plot_title, " (Significant Bands)"),
               subtitle = model_formula_str) +
          theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
                plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 8, face = "italic"))

        return(list(plot = p, contrasts = contrast_tests, plot_type = "three_way_interaction"))

      } else {
        cat("No significant contrasts found after correction\n")
        return(list(plot = NULL, contrasts = contrast_tests, plot_type = "no_significant_contrasts"))
      }

    } else if (two_way_sig) {

      cat("Two-way", paste0(x_var, ":", x_var2),
          "interaction is significant (averaged across", band_var, ")\n")

      emm_formula   <- as.formula(paste0("~ ", x_var, " * ", x_var2))
      contrasts_emm <- emmeans(model, emm_formula, at = x_var_at)
      raw_contrasts <- contrast(contrasts_emm,
                                simple = list(x_var, x_var2),
                                method = "pairwise",
                                adjust = "none")
      contrast_tests <- do.call(rbind, summary(raw_contrasts)) %>%
        as_tibble() %>%
        add_sig_stars()

      print(contrast_tests)

      contrasts_sig <- contrast_tests %>% filter(sig != "")

      if (nrow(contrasts_sig) > 0) {
        p <- ggplot(model_data, aes(x = !!sym(x_var2), y = .fitted, fill = !!sym(x_var))) +
          geom_boxplot(position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), alpha = 0.7) +
          stat_summary(fun = median, geom = "point", size = 3,
                       position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
                       shape = 95, show.legend = FALSE) +
          stat_summary(fun.data = function(x) {
            data.frame(ymin = quantile(x, 0.25), ymax = quantile(x, 0.75))
          }, geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2,
          position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), show.legend = FALSE) +
          geom_point(position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.2, dodge.width = 0.9),
                     alpha = 0.5, size = 1.5) +
          scale_fill_manual(values = event_colors) +
          theme_minimal() +
          labs(x = stringr::str_to_title(x_var2), y = y_label,
               fill = stringr::str_to_title(x_var),
               title = paste0(plot_title, " (Averaged Across ", stringr::str_to_title(band_var), ")"),
               subtitle = model_formula_str) +
          theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
                plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 8, face = "italic"))

        return(list(plot = p, contrasts = contrast_tests, plot_type = "two_way_interaction"))

      } else {
        cat("No significant contrasts found after correction\n")
        return(list(plot = NULL, contrasts = contrast_tests, plot_type = "no_significant_contrasts"))
      }

    } else {
      cat("Neither three-way nor", paste0(x_var, ":", x_var2),
          "two-way interaction is significant — falling back to single-predictor screening on", x_var, "\n")
      x_var2 <- NULL  # fall through to existing logic below
    }
  }

  # ---- CASE: single x_var (original behavior) ----
  interaction_term <- paste0(band_var, ":", x_var)
  # anova row names may be ordered either way depending on formula
  if (!interaction_term %in% rownames(interaction_test)) {
    interaction_term <- paste0(x_var, ":", band_var)
  }
  interaction_sig <- interaction_test[interaction_term, "Pr(>F)"] < alpha
  event_sig       <- interaction_test[x_var,           "Pr(>F)"] < alpha

  if (interaction_sig) {

    cat("Band:event interaction is significant - running contrasts by band\n")

    # Create emmeans formula
    emm_formula <- as.formula(paste0("~ ", x_var, " * ", band_var))

    # Run contrasts
    contrasts_emm <- emmeans(model, emm_formula, at = x_var_at)
    all_contrasts <- contrast(contrasts_emm,
                              simple = x_var,
                              method = 'pairwise',
                              adjust = 'none')
    contrast_tests <- summary(all_contrasts) %>%
      as_tibble() %>%
      add_sig_stars()

    print(contrast_tests)

    # Filter for significant contrasts
    contrasts_sig <- contrast_tests %>%
      filter(sig != "")

    if (nrow(contrasts_sig) > 0) {
      sig_bands <- unique(contrasts_sig[[band_var]])
      plot_data <- model_data %>%
        filter(!!sym(band_var) %in% sig_bands)

      # Create plot by band
      p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = !!sym(band_var), y = .fitted, fill = !!sym(x_var))) +
        geom_boxplot(position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
                     alpha = 0.7) +
        stat_summary(fun = median,
                     geom = "point",
                     size = 3,
                     position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
                     shape = 95,
                     show.legend = FALSE) +
        stat_summary(fun.data = function(x) {
          data.frame(ymin = quantile(x, 0.25),
                     ymax = quantile(x, 0.75))
        },
        geom = "errorbar",
        width = 0.2,
        position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
        show.legend = FALSE) +
        geom_point(position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.2,
                                                    dodge.width = 0.9),
                   alpha = 0.5,
                   size = 1.5) +
        scale_fill_manual(values = event_colors) +
        theme_minimal() +
        labs(x = "Frequency Band",
             y = y_label,
             fill = stringr::str_to_title(x_var),
             title = paste0(plot_title, " (Significant Bands)"),
             subtitle = model_formula_str) +
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
              plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 8, face = "italic"))

      return(list(
        plot = p,
        contrasts = contrast_tests,
        plot_type = "interaction"
      ))

    } else {
      cat("No significant contrasts found after correction\n")
      return(list(
        plot = NULL,
        contrasts = contrast_tests,
        plot_type = "no_significant_contrasts"
      ))
    }

  } else if (event_sig) {

    cat("Only main effect of event is significant - running contrasts averaged across bands\n")

    # Contrasts averaged across bands
    emm_formula <- as.formula(paste0("~ ", x_var))
    event_contrasts <- emmeans(model, emm_formula)
    event_pairwise <- contrast(event_contrasts, method = 'pairwise', adjust = 'none')
    contrast_tests <- summary(event_pairwise) %>%
      as_tibble() %>%
      add_sig_stars()

    print(contrast_tests)

    # Plot averaged across bands
    p <- ggplot(model_data, aes(x = !!sym(x_var), y = .fitted, fill = !!sym(x_var))) +
      geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, width = 0.6) +
      geom_point(position = position_jitter(width = 0.2),
                 alpha = 0.3,
                 size = 1.5) +
      stat_summary(fun = median,
                   geom = "point",
                   size = 4,
                   shape = 95) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = event_colors) +
      theme_minimal() +
      labs(x = stringr::str_to_title(x_var),
           y = y_label,
           fill = stringr::str_to_title(x_var),
           title = paste0(plot_title, " (Averaged Across Bands)"),
           subtitle = model_formula_str) +
      theme(legend.position = "none",
            plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 8, face = "italic"))

    return(list(
      plot = p,
      contrasts = contrast_tests,
      plot_type = "main_effect"
    ))

  } else {
    cat("Neither band:event interaction nor event main effect is significant\n")

    return(list(
      plot = NULL,
      contrasts = NULL,
      plot_type = "no_significant_effects"
    ))
  }
}

analyze_multiple_regions <- function(data_list,
                                    region_names,
                                    fixed_effects,
                                    conserved_random_effects,
                                    test_random_effects,
                                    title = NULL,
                                    event_colors,
                                    y_label = "Fitted Values",
                                    x_var = "event",
                                    x_var2 = NULL,
                                    band_var = "band", 
                                    criterion = "AIC",
                                    REML = FALSE,
                                    alpha = 0.05,
                                    verbose = TRUE) {
  #' Analyze multiple brain regions with model selection and contrasts
  #' 
  #' @param data_list List of dataframes, one per region
  #' @param region_names Character vector of region names (same length as data_list)
  #' @param fixed_effects String with fixed effects formula
  #' @param conserved_random_effects Character vector of random effects to always include
  #' @param test_random_effects Character vector of random effects to test
  #' @param plot_titles Character vector of plot titles (NULL = auto-generate from region_names)
  #' @param event_colors Named vector of colors for events
  #' @param y_label Y-axis label for plots
  #' @param x_var Name of main predictor variable
  #' @param band_var Name of frequency band variable
  #' @param criterion "AIC" or "BIC" for model selection
  #' @param REML Logical for REML vs ML
  #' @param alpha Significance level
  #' @param verbose Print progress messages
  #' 
  #' @return List with: plots (list of ggplots), contrasts (combined tibble), models (list of best models)
  
  # Check inputs
  if (length(data_list) != length(region_names)) {
    stop("data_list and region_names must be the same length")
  }
  
  # Auto-generate plot titles if not provided
  plot_titles <- paste(region_names, title)
  
  
  # Initialize storage
  all_plots <- list()
  all_contrasts <- list()
  all_models <- list()
  all_model_comparisons <- list()
  all_interaction_tests <- list()

  # Set emmeans limits
  max_n <- max(sapply(data_list, nrow))
  limit <- ceiling(max_n * 2)
  emm_options(lmerTest.limit = limit, pbkrtest.limit = limit)
  if (verbose) cat("Set emmeans limits to", limit, "\n\n")
  
  # Loop through each region
  for (i in seq_along(data_list)) {
    
    region <- region_names[i]
    data <- data_list[[i]]
    
    if (verbose) {
      cat("==================================================\n")
      cat("Processing:", region, "\n")
      cat("==================================================\n")
    }
    
    # Step 1: Model selection
    if (verbose) cat("\n--- Running model selection ---\n")
    
    model_selection <- tryCatch({
      select_best_lmer(
        data = data,
        fixed_effects = fixed_effects,
        conserved_random_effects = conserved_random_effects,
        test_random_effects = test_random_effects,
        criterion = criterion,
        REML = REML,
        verbose = verbose
      )
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat("ERROR in model selection for", region, ":", e$message, "\n")
      return(NULL)
    })
    
    if (is.null(model_selection)) {
      cat("Skipping", region, "due to model selection error\n\n")
      next
    }
    
    best_model <- model_selection$best_model  # REML=TRUE
    all_models[[region]] <- best_model
    all_model_comparisons[[region]] <- model_selection$model_results
    all_interaction_tests[[region]] <- model_selection$interaction_test


    # Step 2: Contrast analysis and plotting
    if (verbose) cat("\n--- Running contrast analysis ---\n")

    contrast_results <- tryCatch({
      analyze_and_plot_contrasts(
        model = best_model,
        interaction_test = model_selection$interaction_test,
        plot_title = plot_titles[i],
        event_colors = event_colors,
        y_label = y_label,
        x_var = x_var,
        x_var2 = x_var2,
        band_var = band_var,
        alpha = alpha
      )
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat("ERROR in contrast analysis for", region, ":", e$message, "\n")
      return(NULL)
    })
    
    if (is.null(contrast_results)) {
      cat("Skipping", region, "due to contrast analysis error\n\n")
      next
    }
    
    # Store plot
    all_plots[[region]] <- contrast_results$plot
    
    # Store contrasts with metadata
    if (!is.null(contrast_results$contrasts)) {
      contrasts_with_metadata <- contrast_results$contrasts %>%
        mutate(
          region = region,
          model_formula = paste(deparse(formula(best_model)), collapse = " "),
          plot_type = contrast_results$plot_type,
          .before = 1
        )
      all_contrasts[[region]] <- contrasts_with_metadata
    }
    
    if (verbose) cat("\n")
  }
  
  # Combine all contrasts into one tibble
  combined_contrasts <- bind_rows(all_contrasts)
  
  # Summary message
  if (verbose) {
    cat("\n==================================================\n")
    cat("SUMMARY\n")
    cat("==================================================\n")
    cat("Regions analyzed:", length(all_models), "/", length(region_names), "\n")
    cat("Plots created:", sum(!sapply(all_plots, is.null)), "\n")
    cat("Total contrasts:", nrow(combined_contrasts), "\n")
    cat("Significant contrasts:", sum(combined_contrasts$sig != "", na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
  }
  
  # Return results
  return(list(
    plots             = all_plots,
    contrasts         = combined_contrasts,
    models            = all_models,
    model_comparisons = all_model_comparisons,
    interaction_tests = all_interaction_tests
  ))
}
```



## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r}
df <- read.csv("C:/Users/megha/Documents/GitHub/diff_fam_social_memory_ephys/pilot2/habit_dishabit_phase1/lfp_kinematics_habit_dishabit.csv")
df
all_df <- df |>
  mutate(subject = as_factor(subject),
         recording = as_factor(recording),
         event = as_factor(event),
         partner = as_factor(partner),
         trial = as_factor(trial),
         band = as_factor(band),
         metric = as_factor(metric),
         region_or_pair = as_factor(region_or_pair),
         condition = as_factor(condition)) |>
  mutate(event = factor(event, levels = c("exp1 sniff", "exp2 sniff", "exp3 sniff", "exp4 sniff", "exp5 sniff"))) |>
  mutate(event_length = event_length /10000) |>
  mutate(exposure_num = as.numeric(str_extract(as.character(event), "\\d+"))) |>
  select(-(1)) 

all_df

str(all_df)
 
```

```{r}

power_df  <-  all_df |>
  filter(metric == 'power') |>
  drop_na() 

coherence_df  <- all_df |>
  filter(metric == 'coherence')  |>
  drop_na() 
 
granger_df  <- all_df |>
  filter(metric == 'granger') |>
  drop_na() 
```


```{r}
novel_power_df <- power_df |>
  filter(event %in% c("exp1 sniff", "exp2 sniff", "exp3 sniff", "exp4 sniff"),
         condition == "novel")

cagemate_power_df <- power_df |>
  filter(event %in% c("exp1 sniff", "exp2 sniff", "exp3 sniff", "exp4 sniff"),
         condition == "cagemate")

cagemate_coherence_df <- coherence_df |>
    filter(event %in% c("exp1 sniff", "exp2 sniff", "exp3 sniff", "exp4 sniff"),
         condition == "cagemate")

novel_coherence_df <- coherence_df |>
    filter(event %in% c("exp1 sniff", "exp2 sniff", "exp3 sniff", "exp4 sniff"),
         condition == "novel")

cagemate_granger_df <- granger_df |>
   filter(event %in% c("exp1 sniff", "exp2 sniff", "exp3 sniff", "exp4 sniff"),
         condition == "cagemate")
```

```{r}
both_power_df <- power_df |>
  filter(event %in% c("exp1 sniff", "exp2 sniff", "exp3 sniff", "exp4 sniff"))

both_coherence_df<- coherence_df |>
  filter(event %in% c("exp1 sniff", "exp2 sniff", "exp3 sniff", "exp4 sniff"))

both_granger_df <- granger_df |>
  filter(event %in% c("exp1 sniff", "exp2 sniff", "exp3 sniff", "exp4 sniff"))
```


Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
```{r}

mPFC_novel_power_df <- novel_power_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'mPFC')

NAc_novel_power_df  <- novel_power_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'NAc')

MD_novel_power_df <- novel_power_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'MD')
BLA_novel_power_df  <- novel_power_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'BLA')
vHPC_novel_power_df  <- novel_power_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'vHPC')

data_list <- list(
  mPFC_novel_power_df,
  NAc_novel_power_df,
  MD_novel_power_df,
  BLA_novel_power_df,
  vHPC_novel_power_df
)

region_names <- c("mPFC", "NAc", "MD", "BLA", "vHPC")

# Define colors

event_colors <- c(
  "exp1 sniff" = "#D0D0D0",
  "exp2 sniff" = "#9E9E9E",
  "exp3 sniff"    = "#626262",
  'exp4 sniff' = "#2E2E2E"

)
# event_colors <- c("cagemate" = "#792910",
#                   "novel" = "#15616F")
  

# Run analysis across all regions
novel_results <- analyze_multiple_regions(
  data_list = data_list,
  region_names = region_names,
  fixed_effects = "value ~ band * event + velocity_mouse1 + event_length + (1|recording)",
  conserved_random_effects = "subject",
  test_random_effects = c(),
  event_colors = event_colors,
  y_label = "Fitted Power Values",
  x_var = "event",
  criterion = "AIC",
  REML = FALSE,
  verbose = TRUE
)

# Access novel_results

# View individual plots
print(novel_results$plots$mPFC)
print(novel_results$plots$BLA)
print(novel_results$plots$NAc)
print(novel_results$plots$MD)
print(novel_results$plots$vHPC)

# View combined contrasts table
View(novel_results$contrasts)


```
```{r}
all_plots <- novel_results$plots
all_contrasts <- novel_results$contrasts
all_models <- novel_results$models
your_model <- all_models[["vHPC"]]

model_data <- augment(your_model)

  # Identify outliers per band x agent group
outliers <- model_data %>%
  group_by(band, event) %>%
  mutate(
    q1  = quantile(.fitted, 0.25),
    q3  = quantile(.fitted, 0.75),
    iqr = q3 - q1,
    is_high_outlier = .fitted > q3 + 1.5 * iqr
  ) %>%
  filter(is_high_outlier) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(desc(.fitted))

print(outliers)
```


```{r}
ranova_power_results <- imap_dfr(all_models, function(model, region) {
  ranova(model) %>%
    as_tibble(rownames = "term") %>%
    mutate(region = region, .before = 1)
})

ranova_power_results %>%
  filter(term != "<none>") %>%
  group_by(term) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_p    = mean(`Pr(>Chisq)`, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_p  = median(`Pr(>Chisq)`, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_sig     = sum(`Pr(>Chisq)` < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_total   = n()
  )
```

```{r}

mPFC_cagemate_power_df <- cagemate_power_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'mPFC')

NAc_cagemate_power_df  <- cagemate_power_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'NAc')

MD_cagemate_power_df <- cagemate_power_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'MD')
BLA_cagemate_power_df  <- cagemate_power_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'BLA')
vHPC_cagemate_power_df  <- cagemate_power_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'vHPC')

data_list <- list(
  mPFC_cagemate_power_df,
  NAc_cagemate_power_df,
  MD_cagemate_power_df,
  BLA_cagemate_power_df,
  vHPC_cagemate_power_df
)

region_names <- c("mPFC", "NAc", "MD", "BLA", "vHPC")

# Define colors

event_colors <- c(
  "exp1 sniff" = "#D0D0D0",
  "exp2 sniff" = "#9E9E9E",
  "exp3 sniff"    = "#626262",
  'exp4 sniff' = "#2E2E2E"

)

  

# Run analysis across all regions
cagemate_results <- analyze_multiple_regions(
  data_list = data_list,
  region_names = region_names,
  fixed_effects = "value ~ band * event + velocity_mouse1 + event_length + (1|recording)",
  conserved_random_effects = "subject",
  test_random_effects = c(),
  event_colors = event_colors,
  y_label = "Fitted Power Values",
  x_var = "event",
  criterion = "AIC",
  REML = FALSE,
  verbose = TRUE
)

# Access cagemate_results
all_plots <- cagemate_results$plots
all_contrasts <- cagemate_results$contrasts
all_models <- cagemate_results$models

# View individual plots
print(cagemate_results$plots$mPFC)
print(cagemate_results$plots$BLA)
print(cagemate_results$plots$NAc)
print(cagemate_results$plots$MD)
print(cagemate_results$plots$vHPC)

# View combined contrasts table
View(cagemate_results$contrasts)
```
```{r}

all_models <- cagemate_results$models
your_model <- all_models[["MD"]]

model_data <- augment(your_model)

  # Identify outliers per band x agent group
cage_outliers <- model_data %>%
  group_by(band, event) %>%
  mutate(
    q1  = quantile(.fitted, 0.25),
    q3  = quantile(.fitted, 0.75),
    iqr = q3 - q1,
    is_high_outlier = .fitted > q3 + 1.5 * iqr
  ) %>%
  filter(is_high_outlier) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(desc(.fitted))

print(cage_outliers)
```
```{r}
library(broom.mixed)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)

region_names <- names(novel_results$models)
event_colors <- c("cagemate" = "#792910",
                   "novel" = "#15616F")
plots <- map(region_names, function(region) {
  
  # Extract coefficients from each model
  novel_coef <- tidy(novel_results$models[[region]], effects = "fixed") %>%
    mutate(agent_type = "novel")
  
  cagemate_coef <- tidy(cagemate_results$models[[region]], effects = "fixed") %>%
    mutate(agent_type = "cagemate")
  
  # Combine and filter to exposure_num terms only
  coef_compare <- bind_rows(novel_coef, cagemate_coef) %>%
    filter(str_detect(term, "event")) %>%
    mutate(
      lower = estimate - 1.96 * std.error,
      upper = estimate + 1.96 * std.error,
      agent_type = factor(agent_type, levels = c("cagemate", "novel"))
    )
  
  if (nrow(coef_compare) == 0) {
    message("No exposure_num terms found for region: ", region)
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  ggplot(coef_compare, aes(x = term, y = estimate,
                            color = agent_type,
                            ymin = lower, ymax = upper)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
               color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.5) +
    geom_pointrange(position = position_dodge(width = 0.5),
                    linewidth = 0.8, size = 0.6) +
    scale_color_manual(values = event_colors,
                       labels = c("Cagemate", "Novel")) +
    labs(
      title    = region,
      subtitle = "exposure_num coefficients ± 95% CI",
      x        = "",
      y        = "Coefficient estimate",
      color    = "Agent type"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x     = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold")
    )
})

names(plots) <- region_names

# Print all plots
walk(region_names, function(region) {
  if (!is.null(plots[[region]])) print(plots[[region]])
})
```



```{r}
mPFC_both_power_df <- both_power_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'mPFC')

NAc_both_power_df  <- both_power_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'NAc')

MD_both_power_df <- both_power_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'MD')
BLA_both_power_df  <- both_power_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'BLA')
vHPC_both_power_df  <- both_power_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'vHPC')

data_list <- list(
  mPFC_both_power_df,
  NAc_both_power_df,
  MD_both_power_df,
  BLA_both_power_df,
  vHPC_both_power_df
)

region_names <- c("mPFC", "NAc", "MD", "BLA", "vHPC")

# Define colors

event_colors <- c(
  "exp1 sniff" = "#D0D0D0",
  "exp2 sniff" = "#9E9E9E",
  "exp3 sniff"    = "#626262",
  'exp4 sniff' = "#2E2E2E"

)
# event_colors <- c("both" = "#792910",
#                   "both" = "#15616F")
  

# Run analysis across all regions
both_results <- analyze_multiple_regions(
  data_list = data_list,
  region_names = region_names,
  fixed_effects = "value ~ band * exposure_num * condition + velocity_mouse1 + event_length + (1|recording)",
  conserved_random_effects = "subject",
  test_random_effects = c(),
  event_colors = event_colors,
  y_label = "Fitted Power Values",
  x_var = "exposure_num",
  x_var2 = "condition",
  criterion = "AIC",
  REML = FALSE,
  verbose = TRUE
)

# Access both_results
all_plots <- both_results$plots
all_contrasts <- both_results$contrasts
all_models <- both_results$models

# View individual plots
print(both_results$plots$mPFC)
print(both_results$plots$BLA)
print(both_results$plots$NAc)
print(both_results$plots$MD)
print(both_results$plots$vHPC)

# View combined contrasts table
View(both_results$contrasts)
```

```{r}
both_results$model_comparisons
```


```{r}
library(broom.mixed)
library(emmeans)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(tidyr)

# ── 1. EXTRACT EMTRENDS PER REGION ───────────────────────────────────────────

slope_results <- imap_dfr(both_results$models, function(model, region) {
  emtrends(
    model,
    specs = ~ condition | band,
    var   = "exposure_num"
  ) %>%
    summary(infer = TRUE) %>%
    as_tibble() %>%
    mutate(region = region)
})

# Apply BH correction
slope_results <- slope_results %>%
  mutate(
    p.adjusted = p.adjust(p.value, method = "BH"),
    sig = case_when(
      p.adjusted < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.adjusted < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.adjusted < 0.05  ~ "*",
      p.adjusted < 0.1   ~ ".",
      TRUE               ~ ""
    ),
    # Factor ordering
    band   = factor(band,   levels = c("Delta", "Theta", "Beta",
                                        "Low gamma", "High gamma")),
    region = factor(region, levels = c("mPFC", "NAc", "MD", "BLA", "vHPC"))
  )

print(slope_results %>%
        select(region, band, condition, exposure_num.trend,
               SE, p.adjusted, sig) %>%
        arrange(region, band, condition))

# ── 2. SEPARATE INTO CAGEMATE AND NOVEL ───────────────────────────────────────

slopes_cagemate <- slope_results %>% filter(condition == "cagemate")
slopes_novel    <- slope_results %>% filter(condition == "novel")

# ── 3. HEATMAP FUNCTION ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

plot_slope_heatmap <- function(slopes_df, agent_type, agent_color, title) {

  # Shared color scale limits — use global range across both conditions
  # so heatmaps are directly comparable
  global_lim <- max(abs(slope_results$exposure_num.trend), na.rm = TRUE)

  ggplot(slopes_df,
         aes(x = band, y = region, fill = exposure_num.trend)) +

    geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.8) +

    # Significance stars
    geom_text(aes(label = sig),
              size = 5, fontface = "bold", color = "black") +

    # Diverging scale centered at 0
    # positive = LFP increases across exposures
    # negative = LFP decreases (habituation)
    scale_fill_gradient2(
      low      = "#2166AC",   # blue = decreasing (habituation)
      mid      = "white",
      high     = agent_color, # agent color = increasing
      midpoint = 0,
      limits   = c(-global_lim, global_lim),
      name     = "Exposure\nslope\n(emtrend)"
    ) +

    labs(
      title    = title,
      subtitle = "Slope of LFP across exposures 1–4 | * BH-adjusted p<0.05",
      x        = "Frequency band",
      y        = "Brain region"
    ) +

    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, face = "bold"),
      axis.text.y  = element_text(face = "bold"),
      plot.title   = element_text(face = "bold", color = agent_color, size = 14),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 9, color = "grey40"),
      panel.grid   = element_blank(),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 9)
    )
}

# ── 4. RENDER HEATMAPS ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

p_cagemate <- plot_slope_heatmap(
  slopes_cagemate,
  agent       = "cagemate",
  agent_color = "#792910",
  title       = "Cagemate — LFP exposure slope"
)

p_novel <- plot_slope_heatmap(
  slopes_novel,
  agent       = "novel",
  agent_color = "#15616F",
  title       = "Novel — LFP exposure slope"
)

# Print side by side
library(patchwork)
p_cagemate + p_novel +
  plot_annotation(
    title    = "Habituation trajectories: cagemate vs novel",
    subtitle = "Blue = decreasing LFP across exposures | Color = increasing",
    theme    = theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey40")
    )
  )
```



```{r}
library(broom.mixed)
library(emmeans)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(tidyr)
library(patchwork)

# ── 1. EXTRACT OMNIBUS F-TEST RESULTS PER REGION ─────────────────────────────
# Need to know which interaction terms are significant per region

interaction_lookup <- imap_dfr(both_results$interaction_tests, function(it, region) {
  tibble(
    region                   = region,
    three_way_sig            = "band:event:condition" %in% rownames(it) &&
                               it["band:event:condition", "Pr(>F)"] < 0.05 ||
                               "band:exposure_num:condition" %in% rownames(it) &&
                               it["band:exposure_num:condition", "Pr(>F)"] < 0.05,
    band_condition_sig       = "band:condition" %in% rownames(it) &&
                               it["band:condition", "Pr(>F)"] < 0.05,
    exposure_condition_sig   = any(
      sapply(rownames(it), function(r) {
        grepl("exposure_num", r) && grepl("condition", r) &&
        it[r, "Pr(>F)"] < 0.05
      })
    )
  )
})

print(interaction_lookup)

# ── 2. EXTRACT EMTRENDS PER REGION ───────────────────────────────────────────

get_slopes_for_region <- function(model, region, plot_type) {
  
  if (plot_type == "three_way") {
    emtrends(model, specs = ~ condition | band,
             var = "exposure_num") %>%
      summary(infer = TRUE) %>%
      as_tibble() %>%
      mutate(region = region, band_col = as.character(band))
    
  } else if (plot_type == "band_condition") {
    emtrends(model, specs = ~ condition,
             var = "exposure_num") %>%
      summary(infer = TRUE) %>%
      as_tibble() %>%
      # Expand to all 5 bands so layout matches three_way regions
      tidyr::crossing(band_col = c("Delta", "Theta", "Beta", 
                                   "Low gamma", "High gamma")) %>%
      mutate(region = region)
    
  } else {
    # No significant exposure interaction — grey placeholder for all 5 bands
    tidyr::crossing(
      condition = c("cagemate", "novel"),
      band_col  = c("Delta", "Theta", "Beta", "Low gamma", "High gamma")
    ) %>%
      mutate(
        exposure_num.trend = NA_real_,
        SE                 = NA_real_,
        df                 = NA_real_,
        t.ratio            = NA_real_,
        p.value            = NA_real_,
        lower.CL           = NA_real_,
        upper.CL           = NA_real_,
        region             = region
      )
  }
}

# ── Update factor levels — remove "All Bands" ─────────────────────────────────
slope_results <- slope_results %>%
  mutate(
    p.adjusted = p.adjust(p.value, method = "BH"),
    sig = case_when(
      is.na(p.value)     ~ "",
      p.adjusted < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.adjusted < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.adjusted < 0.05  ~ "*",
      p.adjusted < 0.1   ~ ".",
      TRUE               ~ ""
    ),
    band_col = factor(band,
                      levels = c("Delta", "Theta", "Beta",
                                 "Low gamma", "High gamma")),  # ← no "All Bands"
    region   = factor(region,
                      levels = c("mPFC", "NAc", "MD", "BLA", "vHPC"))
  )

slope_results <- imap_dfr(both_results$models, function(model, region) {
  
  lookup <- interaction_lookup %>% filter(region == !!region)
  
  plot_type <- case_when(
    lookup$three_way_sig          ~ "three_way",
    lookup$band_condition_sig     ~ "band_condition",
    TRUE                          ~ "none"
  )
  
  get_slopes_for_region(model, region, plot_type)
})

# ── 3. APPLY BH CORRECTION ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

slope_results <- slope_results %>%
  mutate(
    p.adjusted = p.adjust(p.value, method = "BH"),
    sig = case_when(
      is.na(p.value)     ~ "",           # grey cells get no star
      p.adjusted < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.adjusted < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.adjusted < 0.05  ~ "*",
      p.adjusted < 0.1   ~ ".",
      TRUE               ~ ""
    ),
    band_col = factor(band_col,
                      levels = c("Delta", "Theta", "Beta",
                                 "Low gamma", "High gamma", "All Bands")),
    region   = factor(region,
                      levels = c("mPFC", "NAc", "MD", "BLA", "vHPC"))
  )

# ── 4. SEPARATE INTO CONDITIONS ───────────────────────────────────────────────

slopes_cagemate <- slope_results %>% filter(condition == "cagemate")
slopes_novel    <- slope_results %>% filter(condition == "novel")

# ── 5. HEATMAP FUNCTION ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

plot_slope_heatmap <- function(slopes_df, agent_color, title) {
  
  global_lim <- max(abs(slope_results$exposure_num.trend), na.rm = TRUE)
  
  ggplot(slopes_df,
         aes(x = band_col, y = region, fill = exposure_num.trend)) +
    
    geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.8,
              aes(alpha = if_else(is.na(exposure_num.trend), 0.15, 1.0))) +
    
    # Grey overlay for NA cells (no significant exposure interaction)
    geom_tile(data = slopes_df %>% filter(is.na(exposure_num.trend)),
              fill = "#CCCCCC", color = "white", linewidth = 0.8) +
    
    # Significance stars
    geom_text(aes(label = sig),
              size = 5, fontface = "bold", color = "black",
              na.rm = TRUE) +
    
    # Diverging color scale
    scale_fill_gradient2(
      low      = "#2166AC",
      mid      = "white",
      high     = agent_color,
      midpoint = 0,
      limits   = c(-global_lim, global_lim),
      name     = "Exposure\nslope\n(emtrend)",
      na.value = "#CCCCCC"    # grey for NA
    ) +
    
    
    scale_alpha_identity() +
    
    labs(
      title    = title,
      # subtitle = "Slope of LFP across exposures | * BH-adjusted p<0.05 | Grey = no sig exposure effect",
      x        = "Frequency band",
      y        = "Brain region"
    ) +
    
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x   = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, face = "bold"),
      axis.text.y   = element_text(face = "bold"),
      plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", color = agent_color, size = 14),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 9, color = "grey40"),
      panel.grid    = element_blank(),
      legend.title  = element_text(size = 9)
    )
}

# ── 6. RENDER ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

p_cagemate <- plot_slope_heatmap(
  slopes_cagemate,
  agent_color = "#792910",
  title       = "Cagemate — LFP exposure slope"
)

p_novel <- plot_slope_heatmap(
  slopes_novel,
  agent_color = "#15616F",
  title       = "Novel — LFP exposure slope"
)

p_cagemate + p_novel +
  plot_annotation(
    title    = "Habituation trajectories: cagemate vs novel",
    # subtitle = "Blue = decreasing LFP | Colored = increasing | Grey = no exposure effect | Dashed line separates band-specific from collapsed",
    theme    = theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 9, color = "grey40")
    )
  )

ggsave("r_stuff/habituation_heatmaps.png",
       width = 16, height = 5, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
```

```{r}
# ── PLOT CAGEMATE vs NOVEL EXPOSURE TRAJECTORY FOR A GIVEN REGION + BAND ──────
#
# Uses emmeans predictions at exposure_num 1–4 from the fitted model, so
# each line represents the population-level fitted trajectory (all subjects
# collapsed), with a 95% CI ribbon.

plot_exposure_slopes_comparison <- function(models,
                                             region,
                                             band_name,
                                             agent_colors = c(cagemate = "#792910",
                                                              novel    = "#15616F"),
                                             metric_label = "LFP Value") {
  #' Plot emmeans fitted values at exposures 1–4 for cagemate vs novel,
  #' with a connecting line (= emtrend slope), CI ribbon, and error bars.
  #'
  #' @param models       Named list of best lmer models (from both_results$models)
  #' @param region       Character — region name matching names(models)
  #' @param band_name    Character — frequency band to plot
  #' @param agent_colors Named vector, must have "cagemate" and "novel"
  #' @param metric_label Y-axis label
  #'
  #' @return ggplot object

  model <- models[[region]]
  if (is.null(model)) {
    warning("No model found for region: ", region)
    return(NULL)
  }

  # ── 1. emmeans at each integer exposure, holding band fixed ─────────────────
  emm_df <- emmeans(
    model,
    ~ condition | exposure_num,
    at = list(exposure_num = 1:4,
              band         = band_name)
  ) %>%
    summary(infer = TRUE) %>%
    as_tibble()

  if (nrow(emm_df) == 0) {
    warning("emmeans returned no rows for region = '", region,
            "', band = '", band_name, "'")
    return(NULL)
  }

  # ── 2. emtrends for slope annotation in subtitle ─────────────────────────────
  trends_df <- emtrends(
    model,
    specs = ~ condition | band,
    var   = "exposure_num",
    at    = list(band = band_name)
  ) %>%
    summary(infer = TRUE) %>%
    as_tibble() %>%
    filter(as.character(band) == band_name) %>%
    mutate(
      sig = case_when(
        p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
        p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
        p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
        p.value < 0.1   ~ ".",
        TRUE            ~ "n.s."
      ),
      label = paste0(condition, ": β = ",
                     round(exposure_num.trend, 4), " ", sig)
    )

  subtitle_text <- paste(trends_df$label, collapse = "   |   ")

  # ── 3. Plot ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  p <- ggplot(emm_df,
              aes(x    = exposure_num,
                  y    = emmean,
                  color = condition,
                  fill  = condition,
                  group = condition)) +

    # CI ribbon (pointwise, interpolated linearly between the 4 exposures)
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower.CL, ymax = upper.CL),
                alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +

    # Fitted line through the 4 emmeans (slope = emtrend β)
    geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +

    # Error bars on each point
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower.CL, ymax = upper.CL),
                  width = 0.12, linewidth = 0.8) +

    # Emmeans points
    geom_point(size = 3.5) +

    scale_color_manual(values = agent_colors) +
    scale_fill_manual(values  = agent_colors) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:4,
                       labels = paste0("Exp ", 1:4)) +

    labs(
      title    = paste0(region, " — ", band_name),
      subtitle = subtitle_text,
      x        = "Exposure",
      y        = metric_label,
      color    = "Condition",
      fill     = "Condition"
    ) +

    theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle   = element_text(size = 9, color = "grey40",
                                     family = "mono")
    )

  return(p)
}

 p <- plot_exposure_slopes_comparison(
    models       = both_results$models,
    region       = "vHPC",
    band_name    = "Beta",
    metric_label = "Fitted LFP Power"
  )
  print(p)
  
ggsave("r_stuff/exposure_slope_example.png",
       width = 6, height = 5, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
```


```{r}
#mPFC_NAc  mPFC_MD   bla_vhpc  mPFC_BLA 
mpfc_nac_both_coherence_df <- both_coherence_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'mPFC_NAc')

mpfc_md_both_coherence_df  <- both_coherence_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'mPFC_MD')

mpfc_bla_both_coherence_df <- both_coherence_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'mPFC_BLA')
mpfc_vhpc_both_coherence_df  <- both_coherence_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'mpfc_vhpc')

# NAc_MD    NAc_vHPC   NAc_BLA  
nac_md_both_coherence_df <- both_coherence_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'NAc_MD')

nac_bla_both_coherence_df  <- both_coherence_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'NAc_BLA')

nac_vhpc_both_coherence_df <- both_coherence_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'NAc_vHPC')
#MD_BLA BLA_vHPC

bla_vhpc_both_coherence_df  <- both_coherence_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'BLA_vHPC')

bla_md_both_coherence_df  <- both_coherence_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'MD_BLA')

#MD_vHPC
md_vhpc_both_coherence_df  <- both_coherence_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'MD_vHPC')


data_list <- list(
  mpfc_nac_both_coherence_df,
  mpfc_md_both_coherence_df,
  mpfc_bla_both_coherence_df,
  mpfc_vhpc_both_coherence_df,
  nac_md_both_coherence_df,
  nac_bla_both_coherence_df,
  nac_vhpc_both_coherence_df,
  bla_md_both_coherence_df,
  bla_vhpc_both_coherence_df,
  md_vhpc_both_coherence_df
)

region_names <- c("mPFC_Nac", "mPFC_MD", "mPFC_BLA", "mPFC_vHPC", "NAc_MD", "NAc_BLA", "NAc_vHPC",
                  "MD_BLA", "BLA_vHPC", "MD_vHPC")

# Define colors
event_colors <- c("cagemate" = "#792910",
                  #"familiar" = "#FFAF00", 
                  "novel" = "#15616F")

# Run analysis across all regions
coherence_results <- analyze_multiple_regions(
  data_list = data_list,
  region_names = region_names,
  fixed_effects = "value ~ band * condition * event + velocity_mouse1 + event_lengtth + (1|recording)",
  title = "Coherence",
  conserved_random_effects = "subject",
  test_random_effects = c(),
  event_colors = event_colors,
  y_label = "Fitted Coherence Values",
  x_var = "event",
  x_var2 = 'condition',
  criterion = "AIC",
  REML = FALSE,
  verbose = TRUE
)

# Access results
all_plots_coherence <- coherence_results$plots
all_contrasts_coherence <- coherence_results$contrasts
all_models_coherence <- coherence_results$models

# View individual plots
print(coherence_results$plots$mPFC_NAc)
print(coherence_results$plots$mPFC_BLA)
print(coherence_results$plots$mPFC_MD)
print(coherence_results$plots$mPFC_vHPC)
print(coherence_results$plots$NAc_BLA)
print(coherence_results$plots$NAc_MD)
print(coherence_results$plots$NAc_vHPC)
print(coherence_results$plots$MD_BLA)
print(coherence_results$plots$MD_vHPC)
print(coherence_results$plots$BLA_vHPC)

# View combined contrasts table
View(coherence_results$contrasts)
```








```{r}
ranova_coherence_results <- imap_dfr(all_models_coherence, function(model, region) {
  ranova(model) %>%
    as_tibble(rownames = "term") %>%
    mutate(region = region, .before = 1)
})

```

```{r}
ranova_coherence_results %>%
  filter(term != "<none>") %>%
  group_by(term) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_p    = mean(`Pr(>Chisq)`, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_p  = median(`Pr(>Chisq)`, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_sig     = sum(`Pr(>Chisq)` < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_total   = n()
  )
```

```{r}
#from mpfc
mpfc_nac_cage_v_novel_granger_df <- cage_v_novel_granger_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'mPFC_to_NAc')
mpfc_md_cage_v_novel_granger_df  <- cage_v_novel_granger_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'mPFC_to_MD')
mpfc_bla_cage_v_novel_granger_df <- cage_v_novel_granger_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'mPFC_to_BLA')
mpfc_vhpc_cage_v_novel_granger_df  <- cage_v_novel_granger_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'mPFC_to_vHPC')


# from nac
nac_mpfc_cage_v_novel_granger_df <- cage_v_novel_granger_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'NAc_to_mPFC')
nac_md_cage_v_novel_granger_df <- cage_v_novel_granger_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'NAc_to_MD')
nac_bla_cage_v_novel_granger_df  <- cage_v_novel_granger_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'NAc_to_BLA')
nac_vhpc_cage_v_novel_granger_df <- cage_v_novel_granger_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'NAc_to_vHPC')

# from bla
bla_mpfc_cage_v_novel_granger_df  <- cage_v_novel_granger_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'BLA_to_mPFC')
bla_nac_cage_v_novel_granger_df  <- cage_v_novel_granger_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'BLA_to_NAc')
bla_md_cage_v_novel_granger_df  <- cage_v_novel_granger_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'BLA_to_MD')
bla_vhpc_cage_v_novel_granger_df  <- cage_v_novel_granger_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'BLA_to_vHPC')



# from md 
md_mpfc_cage_v_novel_granger_df  <- cage_v_novel_granger_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'MD_to_mPFC')
md_nac_cage_v_novel_granger_df  <- cage_v_novel_granger_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'MD_to_NAc')
md_bla_cage_v_novel_granger_df  <- cage_v_novel_granger_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'MD_to_BLA')
md_vhpc_cage_v_novel_granger_df  <- cage_v_novel_granger_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'MD_to_vHPC')

# from vHPC
vhpc_mpfc_cage_v_novel_granger_df  <- cage_v_novel_granger_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'vHPC_to_mPFC')
vhpc_nac_cage_v_novel_granger_df  <- cage_v_novel_granger_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'vHPC_to_NAc')
vhpc_bla_cage_v_novel_granger_df  <- cage_v_novel_granger_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'vHPC_to_BLA')
vhpc_md_cage_v_novel_granger_df  <- cage_v_novel_granger_df |>
  filter(region_or_pair == 'vHPC_to_MD')
granger_data_list <- list(
  # From mPFC
  mPFC_to_NAc = mpfc_nac_cage_v_novel_granger_df,
  mPFC_to_MD = mpfc_md_cage_v_novel_granger_df,
  mPFC_to_BLA = mpfc_bla_cage_v_novel_granger_df,
  mPFC_to_vHPC = mpfc_vhpc_cage_v_novel_granger_df,
  
  # From NAc
  NAc_to_mPFC = nac_mpfc_cage_v_novel_granger_df,
  NAc_to_MD = nac_md_cage_v_novel_granger_df,
  NAc_to_BLA = nac_bla_cage_v_novel_granger_df,
  NAc_to_vHPC = nac_vhpc_cage_v_novel_granger_df,
  
  # From BLA
  BLA_to_mPFC = bla_mpfc_cage_v_novel_granger_df,
  BLA_to_NAc = bla_nac_cage_v_novel_granger_df,
  BLA_to_MD = bla_md_cage_v_novel_granger_df,
  BLA_to_vHPC = bla_vhpc_cage_v_novel_granger_df,
  
  # From MD
  MD_to_mPFC = md_mpfc_cage_v_novel_granger_df,
  MD_to_NAc = md_nac_cage_v_novel_granger_df,
  MD_to_BLA = md_bla_cage_v_novel_granger_df,
  MD_to_vHPC = md_vhpc_cage_v_novel_granger_df,
  
  # From vHPC
  vHPC_to_mPFC = vhpc_mpfc_cage_v_novel_granger_df,
  vHPC_to_NAc = vhpc_nac_cage_v_novel_granger_df,
  vHPC_to_BLA = vhpc_bla_cage_v_novel_granger_df,
  vHPC_to_MD = vhpc_md_cage_v_novel_granger_df
)
granger_results <- analyze_multiple_regions(
  data_list = granger_data_list,
  region_names = names(granger_data_list),
  fixed_effects = "value ~ band * condition + velocity_mouse1 + (1|recording) + (1|partner)",
  conserved_random_effects = "subject",
  title = "Granger",
  test_random_effects = c(),
  x_var = "condition",
  event_colors = event_colors,
  y_label = "Fitted Granger Causality Values",
  criterion = "AIC",
  verbose = TRUE
)

all_plots_granger <- granger_results$plots
all_contrasts_granger <- granger_results$contrasts
all_models_granger <- granger_results$models

print(granger_results$plots$mPFC_to_NAc)
print(granger_results$plots$mPFC_to_MD)
print(granger_results$plots$mPFC_to_BLA)
print(granger_results$plots$mPFC_to_vHPC)

print(granger_results$plots$NAc_to_mPFC)
print(granger_results$plots$NAc_to_MD)
print(granger_results$plots$NAc_to_BLA)
print(granger_results$plots$NAc_to_vHPC)

print(granger_results$plots$BLA_to_mPFC)
print(granger_results$plots$BLA_to_NAc)
print(granger_results$plots$BLA_to_MD)
print(granger_results$plots$BLA_to_vHPC)

print(granger_results$plots$MD_to_mPFC)
print(granger_results$plots$MD_to_NAc)
print(granger_results$plots$MD_to_BLA)
print(granger_results$plots$MD_to_vHPC)

print(granger_results$plots$vHPC_to_mPFC)
print(granger_results$plots$vHPC_to_NAc)
print(granger_results$plots$vHPC_to_BLA)
print(granger_results$plots$vHPC_to_MD)

View(granger_results$contrasts)
```

```{r}
ranova_granger_results <- imap_dfr(all_models_granger, function(model, region) {
  ranova(model) %>%
    as_tibble(rownames = "term") %>%
    mutate(region = region, .before = 1)
})

ranova_granger_results %>%
  filter(term != "<none>") %>%
  group_by(term) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_p    = mean(`Pr(>Chisq)`, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_p  = median(`Pr(>Chisq)`, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_sig     = sum(`Pr(>Chisq)` < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_total   = n()
  )
```

```{r}
# ── GLOBAL BH CORRECTION ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Call this AFTER analyze_multiple_regions returns, once per metric
apply_global_bh <- function(results) {
  # results$contrasts already has raw p.value column
  # re-adjust globally across all regions
  results$contrasts <- results$contrasts %>%
    mutate(
      p.value.adjusted = p.adjust(p.value, method = "BH"),
      sig = case_when(
        p.value.adjusted < 0.001 ~ "***",
        p.value.adjusted < 0.01  ~ "**",
        p.value.adjusted < 0.05  ~ "*",
        p.value.adjusted < 0.1   ~ ".",
        TRUE                     ~ ""
      )
    )
  return(results)
}

# Apply after each metric run:
results         <- apply_global_bh(results)          # power
coherence_results <- apply_global_bh(coherence_results)
granger_results   <- apply_global_bh(granger_results)
```



```{r}
# ── RAW DATA + EMMEANS PLOT FUNCTION ─────────────────────────────────────────

event_colors <- c(
  "cagemate" = "#792910",
  "familiar" = "#FFAF00",
  "novel"    = "#15616F"
)

plot_raw_with_emm <- function(region,
                               df_raw,
                               model,
                               interaction_test,
                               contrasts,          # ← add this: the global-BH-adjusted contrasts
                               metric_label = "Power",
                               event_colors,
                               alpha = 0.05,
                               band_var = "band",
                               x_var   = "event") {

  # ── 1. Determine plot type from F-test ──────────────────────────────────────
  interaction_term <- paste0(band_var, ":", x_var)
  if (!interaction_term %in% rownames(interaction_test))
    interaction_term <- paste0(x_var, ":", band_var)

  interaction_sig <- interaction_test[interaction_term, "Pr(>F)"] < alpha
  event_sig       <- interaction_test[x_var,           "Pr(>F)"] < alpha

  if (!interaction_sig && !event_sig) {
    message("Region ", region, ": no significant effects — skipping")
    return(NULL)
  }

  # ── 2. Get significant bands from BH-adjusted contrasts ────────────────────
  region_contrasts <- contrasts %>%
    filter(region == !!region, sig != "")

  if (nrow(region_contrasts) == 0) {
    message("Region ", region, ": no significant contrasts after BH — skipping")
    return(NULL)
  }

  if (interaction_sig) {
    # Only keep bands that have at least one significant contrast
    sig_bands <- unique(region_contrasts[[band_var]])

    raw <- df_raw %>%
      filter(region_or_pair == region,
             event != "empty",
             !!sym(band_var) %in% sig_bands) %>%   # ← filter to sig bands only
      droplevels()

    emm <- emmeans(model, as.formula(paste0("~ ", x_var, " * ", band_var))) %>%
      as_tibble() %>%
      rename(event_col = all_of(x_var),
             band_col  = all_of(band_var)) %>%
      filter(band_col %in% sig_bands)              # ← filter emmeans to match

  } else {
    # Main effect: no band filter needed, just one panel
    raw <- df_raw %>%
      filter(region_or_pair == region,
             event != "empty") %>%
      mutate(band_col = "All Bands") %>%
      droplevels()

    emm <- emmeans(model, as.formula(paste0("~ ", x_var))) %>%
      as_tibble() %>%
      rename(event_col = all_of(x_var)) %>%
      mutate(band_col = "All Bands")
  }



  # ── 4. Scale emmeans onto raw y-axis for dual-axis overlay ──────────────────
  raw_range <- range(raw$value, na.rm = TRUE)
  emm_range <- range(emm$emmean, na.rm = TRUE)

  scale_emm_to_raw <- function(x) {
    (x - emm_range[1]) / diff(emm_range) * diff(raw_range) + raw_range[1]
  }
  scale_raw_to_emm <- function(x) {
    (x - raw_range[1]) / diff(raw_range) * diff(emm_range) + emm_range[1]
  }

  cap_width <- 0.2

  # Compute x positions for emmeans brackets
  if (interaction_sig) {
    band_levels <- levels(raw$band)
    emm <- emm %>%
      mutate(
        emmean_scaled = scale_emm_to_raw(emmean),
        lower_scaled  = scale_emm_to_raw(lower.CL),
        upper_scaled  = scale_emm_to_raw(upper.CL),
        x_pos         = as.numeric(factor(event_col,
                                          levels = c("cagemate", "familiar", "novel"))),
        x_left        = x_pos - cap_width,
        x_right       = x_pos + cap_width
      )
  } else {
    emm <- emm %>%
      mutate(
        emmean_scaled = scale_emm_to_raw(emmean),
        x_pos         = as.numeric(factor(event_col,
                                          levels = c("cagemate", "familiar", "novel"))),
        x_left        = x_pos - cap_width,
        x_right       = x_pos + cap_width
      )
  }

  # ── 5. Build plot ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if (interaction_sig) {

    # Rename columns to match aes
    raw <- raw %>% rename(event_col = all_of(x_var),
                          band_col  = all_of(band_var))

    p <- ggplot(raw, aes(x = event_col, y = value, fill = event_col)) +

      geom_boxplot(
        outlier.size = 0.8,
        width        = 0.5,
        linewidth    = 0.8,
        color        = "black"
      ) +

      # Emmeans bracket — horizontal bar
      geom_segment(data = emm,
                   aes(x = x_left, xend = x_right,
                       y = emmean_scaled, yend = emmean_scaled),
                   inherit.aes = FALSE,
                   color = "#FF1F04", linewidth = 1.1) +

      # Left cap
      geom_segment(data = emm,
                   aes(x = x_left, xend = x_left,
                       y = emmean_scaled - diff(raw_range) * 0.015,
                       yend = emmean_scaled + diff(raw_range) * 0.015),
                   inherit.aes = FALSE,
                   color = "#FF1F04", linewidth = 1.1) +

      # Right cap
      geom_segment(data = emm,
                   aes(x = x_right, xend = x_right,
                       y = emmean_scaled - diff(raw_range) * 0.015,
                       yend = emmean_scaled + diff(raw_range) * 0.015),
                   inherit.aes = FALSE,
                   color = "#FF1F04", linewidth = 1.1) +

      facet_wrap(~ band_col, scales = "free_y", ncol = 5) +

      scale_fill_manual(values = event_colors) +
      scale_x_discrete(labels = NULL) +

      scale_y_continuous(
        name     = metric_label,
        sec.axis = sec_axis(
          transform = scale_raw_to_emm,
          name      = "Estimated Marginal Means",
          labels    = scales::label_comma(accuracy = 0.01)
        )
      ) +

      labs(title = paste0(region, " — ", metric_label),
           x     = "") +

      theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
      theme(
        legend.position    = "none",
        strip.text         = element_text(face = "bold"),
        axis.title.y.right = element_text(color = "#FF1F04", size = 12),
        axis.text.y.right  = element_text(color = "#FF1F04", size = 10),
        axis.ticks.y.right = element_line(color = "#FF1F04"),
        axis.line.y.right  = element_line(color = "#FF1F04"),
        axis.title.y       = element_text(size = 14),
        axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text.x        = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x       = element_blank()
      )

  } else {
    # Main effect only — single panel, events on x axis
    raw <- raw %>% rename(event_col = all_of(x_var))

    p <- ggplot(raw, aes(x = event_col, y = value, fill = event_col)) +

      geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.8, width = 0.5,
                   linewidth = 0.8, color = "black") +

      geom_segment(data = emm,
                   aes(x = x_left, xend = x_right,
                       y = emmean_scaled, yend = emmean_scaled),
                   inherit.aes = FALSE,
                   color = "#FF1F04", linewidth = 1.1) +
      geom_segment(data = emm,
                   aes(x = x_left, xend = x_left,
                       y = emmean_scaled - diff(raw_range) * 0.015,
                       yend = emmean_scaled + diff(raw_range) * 0.015),
                   inherit.aes = FALSE,
                   color = "#FF1F04", linewidth = 1.1) +
      geom_segment(data = emm,
                   aes(x = x_right, xend = x_right,
                       y = emmean_scaled - diff(raw_range) * 0.015,
                       yend = emmean_scaled + diff(raw_range) * 0.015),
                   inherit.aes = FALSE,
                   color = "#FF1F04", linewidth = 1.1) +

      scale_fill_manual(values = event_colors) +
      scale_x_discrete(labels = NULL) +

      scale_y_continuous(
        name     = metric_label,
        sec.axis = sec_axis(
          transform = scale_raw_to_emm,
          name      = "Estimated Marginal Means",
          labels    = scales::label_comma(accuracy = 0.01)
        )
      ) +

      labs(title = paste0(region, " — ", metric_label, " (avg across bands)"),
           x     = "") +

      theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
      theme(
        legend.position    = "none",
        axis.title.y.right = element_text(color = "#FF1F04", size = 12),
        axis.text.y.right  = element_text(color = "#FF1F04", size = 10),
        axis.ticks.y.right = element_line(color = "#FF1F04"),
        axis.line.y.right  = element_line(color = "#FF1F04"),
        axis.title.y       = element_text(size = 14),
        axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text.x        = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x       = element_blank()
      )
  }

  return(p)

}

# ── LOOP OVER ALL POWER REGIONS ──────────────────────────────────────────────

raw_plots <- imap(results$models, function(model, region) {
  it <- results$interaction_tests[[region]]

  plot_raw_with_emm(
    region           = region,
    df_raw           = power_df,
    model            = model,
    interaction_test = it,
    contrasts        = results$contrasts,   # ← pass the global-BH-adjusted contrasts
    metric_label     = "Power",
    event_colors     = event_colors
  )
})

# Print and save
iwalk(raw_plots, function(p, region) {
  if (is.null(p)) return(NULL)
  print(p)
  ggsave(
    paste0("r_stuff/power_analysis/raw_plots/", region, "_raw.png"),
    plot = p, width = 14, height = 5, dpi = 300
  )
})
```

```{r}
raw_plots <- imap(coherence_results$models, function(model, region) {
  it <- coherence_results$interaction_tests[[region]]

  plot_raw_with_emm(
    region           = region,
    df_raw           = coherence_df,
    model            = model,
    interaction_test = it,
    contrasts        = coherence_results$contrasts,   # ← pass the global-BH-adjusted contrasts
    metric_label     = "Coherence",
    event_colors     = event_colors
  )
})

# Print and save
iwalk(raw_plots, function(p, region) {
  if (is.null(p)) return(NULL)
  print(p)
  ggsave(
    paste0("r_stuff/coherence_analysis/raw_plots/", region, "_raw.png"),
    plot = p, width = 14, height = 5, dpi = 300
  )
})
```
```{r}
coherence_results$contrasts |>
  filter(region == "mPFC_Nac",
         sig != "") |>
  select(region, band, contrast, sig)
```

```{r}
raw_plots <- imap(granger_results$models, function(model, region) {
  it <- granger_results$interaction_tests[[region]]

  plot_raw_with_emm(
    region           = region,
    df_raw           = granger_df,
    model            = model,
    interaction_test = it,
    contrasts        = granger_results$contrasts,   # ← pass the global-BH-adjusted contrasts
    metric_label     = "Granger",
    event_colors     = event_colors
  )
})

# Print and save
iwalk(raw_plots, function(p, region) {
  if (is.null(p)) return(NULL)
  print(p)
  ggsave(
    paste0("r_stuff/granger_analysis/raw_plots/", region, "_raw.png"),
    plot = p, width = 14, height = 5, dpi = 300
  )
})
```

```{r}
power_emm_df <- imap_dfr(results$models, function(model, region) {
  emmeans(model, ~ condition * band) %>% as_tibble() %>% mutate(region = region)
})
write_csv(power_emm_df, "r_stuff/cage_v_nov_power_emm.csv")

coherence_emm_df <- imap_dfr(coherence_results$models, function(model, region) {
  emmeans(model, ~ condition * band) %>% as_tibble() %>% mutate(region = region)
})
write_csv(coherence_emm_df, "r_stuff/cage_v_nov_coherence_emm.csv")

granger_emm_df <- imap_dfr(granger_results$models, function(model, region) {
  emmeans(model, ~ condition * band) %>% as_tibble() %>% mutate(region = region)
})

write_csv(coherence_emm_df, "r_stuff/cage_v_nov_granger_emm.csv")

write_csv(results$contrasts,           "r_stuff/cage_v_nov_power_contrasts.csv")
write_csv(coherence_results$contrasts, "r_stuff/cage_v_nov_coherence_contrasts.csv")
write_csv(granger_results$contrasts,   "r_stuff/cage_v_nov_granger_contrasts.csv")

imap_dfr(results$interaction_tests, function(it, region) {
  as.data.frame(it) %>% as_tibble(rownames = "term") %>% mutate(region = region, .before = 1)
}) %>% write_csv("r_stuff/cage_v_nov_power_kr_omnibus.csv")

imap_dfr(coherence_results$interaction_tests, function(it, region) {
  as.data.frame(it) %>% as_tibble(rownames = "term") %>% mutate(region = region, .before = 1)
}) %>% write_csv("r_stuff/cage_v_nov_coherence_kr_omnibus.csv")

imap_dfr(granger_results$interaction_tests, function(it, region) {
  as.data.frame(it) %>% as_tibble(rownames = "term") %>% mutate(region = region, .before = 1)
}) %>% write_csv("r_stuff/cage_v_nov_granger_kr_omnibus.csv")
```




```{r}
write_csv(granger_results$contrasts, "r_stuff/granger_analysis/granger_contrasts_results.csv")
for (pair_name in names(granger_results$plots)) {
  if (!is.null(granger_results$plots[[pair_name]])) {
    ggsave(
      filename = file.path("r_stuff/granger_analysis/granger_plots", paste0(pair_name, ".png")),
      plot = granger_results$plots[[pair_name]],
      width = 10, 
      height = 6, 
      dpi = 300
    )
  }
}
all_model_comparisons <- bind_rows(
  lapply(names(granger_results$model_comparisons), function(region) {
    granger_results$model_comparisons[[region]] %>%
      mutate(region = region, .before = 1)
  })
)
write_csv(all_model_comparisons, 
          "r_stuff/granger_analysis/model_comparisons.csv")

saveRDS(granger_results$models, "r_stuff/granger_analysis/best_granger_models.rds")


```

```{r}

write_csv(coherence_results$contrasts, "r_stuff/coherence_analysis/coherence_contrasts_results.csv")
for (pair_name in names(coherence_results$plots)) {
  if (!is.null(coherence_results$plots[[pair_name]])) {
    ggsave(
      filename = file.path("r_stuff/coherence_analysis/coherence_plots", paste0(pair_name, ".png")),
      plot = coherence_results$plots[[pair_name]],
      width = 10, 
      height = 6, 
      dpi = 300
    )
  }
}
all_model_comparisons <- bind_rows(
  lapply(names(coherence_results$model_comparisons), function(region) {
    coherence_results$model_comparisons[[region]] %>%
      mutate(region = region, .before = 1)
  })
)
write_csv(all_model_comparisons, 
          "r_stuff/coherence_analysis/model_comparisons.csv")

saveRDS(coherence_results$models, "r_stuff/coherence_analysis/best_coherence_models.rds")
```
```{r}
write_csv(results$contrasts, "r_stuff/power_analysis/power_contrasts_results.csv")
for (pair_name in names(results$plots)) {
  if (!is.null(results$plots[[pair_name]])) {
    ggsave(
      filename = file.path("r_stuff/power_analysis/power_plots", paste0(pair_name, ".png")),
      plot = results$plots[[pair_name]],
      width = 10, 
      height = 6, 
      dpi = 300
    )
  }
}
all_model_comparisons <- bind_rows(
  lapply(names(results$model_comparisons), function(region) {
    results$model_comparisons[[region]] %>%
      mutate(region = region, .before = 1)
  })
)
write_csv(all_model_comparisons, 
          "r_stuff/power_analysis/model_comparisons.csv")

saveRDS(results$models, "r_stuff/power_analysis/best_power_models.rds")
```
